Ad Text:
Work closely with executive leadership and play a key role in shaping the financial strategy of a high-impact, fast-moving business.

Take control of budgeting, forecasting, and performance reporting across multiple business units in a visible, leadership-level role

Be a part of a large, multi-national market leader to grow your career.

A dynamic and nationally recognised media brand is seeking a commercially minded and hands-on FP&A Manager to lead its financial planning and performance function. This is a highly visible leadership opportunity in a fast-paced, consumer-driven business, ideal for someone who thrives on strategic influence, executive interaction, and continuous improvement.

You will lead a small high-performing team, take full ownership of budgeting and forecasting cycles, and deliver meaningful insights that shape strategic decisions across the business.


Key Responsibilities

Reporting to the Finance Director, the FP&A Manager will:

Lead group-wide planning, forecasting and reporting cycles, delivering clear performance insights

Craft executive-level narratives explaining key drivers, risks and opportunities

Partner with ELT and finance leadership on profitability, cost optimisation and strategic decisions

Consolidate insights across business units to provide a holistic group view

Deliver ad hoc analysis for senior stakeholders locally and globally

Drive system enhancements, automation and best-practice FP&A processes

 

About You

CA/CPA qualified, with 6 - 8+ years’ experience in commercial finance and audit

Proven leadership experience and stakeholder engagement skills

Advanced Excel and enterprise reporting systems (e.g. TM1, OneStream)

Strong commercial mindset and excellent written and verbal communication skills

New Role – play a key part within our Transformation team




About the opportunity

The AI Business Improvement Specialist plays a pivotal role in strengthening how our AI Strategy team and AI Streams operate. You will establish fit‑for‑purpose frameworks and processes that enable clarity, accountability, and effective prioritisation across a busy and rapidly evolving program of work.

Working across multiple AI initiatives, you’ll support self‑organising, cross‑functional teams drawn from business and technical groups. Your work will ensure alignment to strategic goals, smooth delivery of outcomes, and effective stakeholder engagement. You will also apply analytical thinking to evaluate AI opportunities and contribute to the development of high‑quality business cases.

This is a unique chance to support RACV’s AI ambitions and build organisational readiness for a future powered by intelligent systems.

 

A snapshot of what you’ll be doing 

Lead end-to-end delivery planning processes, and outcome management.

Bring structure to teams through clear communication, coordination and collaboration.

Establish and run fit‑for‑purpose planning and delivery rituals, including quarterly planning and sprint processes.

Support the Head of AI and AI Stream Product Owners in delivering priority initiatives.

Promote built‑in quality, continuous improvement and sustained business value flow.

Foster a culture of accountability, innovation and high performance.

Build strong cross-functional relationships that enhance delivery and support successful AI-  driven outcomes.

Partner with the AI Value Creation Office Lead to provide project visibility, communicate updates, and ensure effective governance.

Support the team by helping to manage competing priorities and minimising uncontrolled work inflow

Assess potential AI use case opportunities in collaboration with business and technical stakeholders.

Gather requirements, manage change impacts, and coordinate timing, training, and support needs.

Complete cost/benefit and risk analyses for proposed improvements.

Contribute to the development of business cases and recommendations.




What you’ll bring 

Strong analytical skills.

Demonstrated project management expertise.

Excellent interpersonal, relationship‑building and networking ability.

Proven facilitation, influencing and negotiation skills.

Strong written communication and numerical analysis capability.

Effective time management and prioritisation skills




 Qualifications & experience 

Tertiary qualifications in commerce, business, IT or a related field.

At least two years’ experience as a business analyst or similar analytical role.

Minimum two years’ experience working on projects with diverse stakeholder groups

Formal project management training or certification (desirable)




There’s so much on offer when you join RACV with benefits including 

Generous discounts on home and car insurance policies and RACV roadside care

Great discounts across all RACV resorts

Discounts from corporate partners that are updated monthly.

Role Summary

We are seeking two experienced Change Analysts to support the successful adoption of a new enterprise solution within a complex environment. In this 12-month contract, you will focus on assessing change impacts, supporting business readiness, and ensuring stakeholders are seamlessly transitioned to new systems and processes. This is an excellent opportunity for a people-centered professional to contribute to a large-scale digital transformation and deliver consistent, high-quality change outcomes.


What You Will Do:

Conduct detailed change impact assessments across business areas, roles, processes, and systems.

Support the development and reporting of business readiness assessments and go-live criteria.

Contribute to the creation of change management artefacts, including adoption plans and learning materials.

Facilitate stakeholder workshops, briefings, and engagement activities to drive adoption.

Assist in the development and deployment of learning assets such as quick reference guides and job aids.


What You Need to Apply for This Role:

Minimum of 5 years of experience in a similar change analyst capacity.

Demonstrated experience supporting organisational change initiatives within project environments.

Proven ability to conduct change impact assessments and support business readiness activities.

Exceptional stakeholder management, collaboration, and facilitation skills.

Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly PowerPoint, for creating change assets.

Desirable Requirement:
Experience in using a range of software products such as SharePoint Online administration, Adobe Creative Suite,

Microsoft Visio, Viva Engage, Forms, and so on.

Interested? Apply Now! If you are a Change Analyst with experience in change impact assessments and business readiness, we want to hear from you. Click "Apply Now". Alternatively, please reach out to Diane for more information - diane.botje@randstaddigital.com.au

Why this role exists
You’ll be partnering directly with a highly strategic Chief Risk Officer (20+ years experience) who operates at altitude — focused on vision, commercial outcomes, and where the business is going next.

We are working with a high-growth fintech as our client located in the Sydney CBD to identify a trusted operator and strategic thought partner. Someone who can:

Translate strategy into execution
Bring structure, clarity, and momentum to complex initiatives
Take ownership of the day-to-day running of the Risk function
The opportunity
You will sit at the centre of the Risk function — owning execution, driving alignment, and ensuring strategic initiatives are delivered. This is a high-impact, high-trust role with exposure across:

Executive leadership
Cross-functional teams
External stakeholders (e.g. regulators, credit bureaus)
You’ll take complex ideas and turn them into clear plans, structured programs, and real outcomes. Does this sound like YOU?

Strategy -> Execution

Translate the CRO’s vision into actionable initiatives and delivery plans
Drive key strategic programs (e.g. automation, process redesign, underwriting improvements)
Ensure alignment between risk strategy and broader commercial priorities
Own the engine room

Run the day-to-day operations of the Risk function
Bring structure, prioritisation, and clarity across multiple initiatives
Diagnose friction points and drive solutions to accelerate delivery
Program & transformation leadership
Lead & collaborate in cross-divisional initiatives and turn ambiguity into clear execution roadmaps including:

Automation of decisioning
Process redesign across application → approval journeys
Organisational transformation as the business scales
Executive partnership

Act as a proxy for the CRO in forums and decision-making
Prepare high-quality materials (slides, insights, reporting) for exec and board audiences
Engage in rigorous, constructive debate with senior stakeholders
Governance & coordination

Oversee reporting, dashboards, and governance cadence
Coordinate with regulators, though limited day-to-day involvement
Manage key external relationships (credit bureaus, partners)
What we’re looking for
We’re not looking for a traditional risk specialist. We’re looking for someone who can think, structure, challenge, and execute.

Ideal background

Management consulting or strategy & operations roles
Experience working closely with senior executives
Exposure to transformation, operations, or program delivery
Financial services experience is helpful — but not essential
Capabilities that matter

Exceptional problem-solving and clarity of thought
Ability to turn complex ideas into structured plans
Strong communication — especially via slides and storytelling
High attention to detail and operational discipline
Confidence to challenge, debate, and influence at senior levels
Hands-on day to day delivery!
You’ll thrive if you:

Enjoy turning ambiguity into action
Are comfortable operating as a “right hand” to a senior leader
Can balance big-picture thinking with hands-on execution
Have the resilience and pragmatism to operate in a fast-moving environment
The bottom line
This is a rare opportunity to step into a role where you will:

Operate at the intersection of strategy, execution, and leadership
Be the force multiplier for a senior executive
Shape how a high-growth fintech scales its risk function
If you’re looking for a role where you can drive real impact, influence at the top table, and build something meaningful, I’d love to speak. Please apply or reach out kylie.saunders@worktrybe.com for a confidential conversation.
